description:
You are logged in as the non-root user “user”.
    A small data set that must be archived is already present in:

    /home/user/data_to_backup/
        ├── docs/
        │   └── report.txt
        ├── images/
        │   ├── photo1.jpg
        │   └── photo2.jpg
        └── scripts/
            └── cleanup.sh

    An empty directory that will represent the “remote host” is also provided:

        /home/user/remote_host/

    Goal
    ----
    1. Using ONE single terminal command, synchronise the entire content of
       /home/user/data_to_backup/ into the destination directory

       /home/user/remote_host/backups/20230115/

       (Create any intermediate directories that do not yet exist.)

    2. After the command finishes, create the text file

       /home/user/backup_logs/backup_20230115.log

       The file must contain **exactly** the four relative paths (one per line,
       in this order and with no extra whitespace or blank lines):

           docs/report.txt
           images/photo1.jpg
           images/photo2.jpg
           scripts/cleanup.sh

       No additional lines, carriage returns or spaces are allowed.  This log
       file will be used by automated grading.

    Verification requirements
    -------------------------
    • Every file listed above must be present inside 
      /home/user/remote_host/backups/20230115/ and must be byte-for-byte
      identical to its counterpart under /home/user/data_to_backup/.

    • The directory tree under /home/user/remote_host/backups/20230115/ must
      preserve the original sub-directory structure (docs, images, scripts).

    • The log file /home/user/backup_logs/backup_20230115.log must exist and
      contain exactly the four lines specified, with Unix line endings (LF).

    • Only a single shell command is allowed to perform the actual file
      transfer; any additional steps are limited to creating the required
      log file.

    Once all criteria are met, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial file system state BEFORE the agent begins:
    --------------------------------------------------
    /home/user/data_to_backup/docs/report.txt
        Content (11 bytes):
            Quarterly
            (LF)
    /home/user/data_to_backup/images/photo1.jpg
        Content (20 bytes):
            JPEG_DUMMY_DATA_1(LF)
    /home/user/data_to_backup/images/photo2.jpg
        Content (20 bytes):
            JPEG_DUMMY_DATA_2(LF)
    /home/user/data_to_backup/scripts/cleanup.sh
        Content (29 bytes):
            #!/bin/bash(LF)echo cleanup(LF)

    /home/user/remote_host/          (directory, empty)
    /home/user/backup_logs/          (directory, empty or absent)

    Expected file system state AFTER correct completion:
    ----------------------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/remote_host/backups/20230115/ exists with the
       following hierarchy and byte-for-byte identical files:

       /home/user/remote_host/backups/20230115/docs/report.txt
           Content identical to original report.txt (11 bytes)
       /home/user/remote_host/backups/20230115/images/photo1.jpg
           Content identical to original photo1.jpg (20 bytes)
       /home/user/remote_host/backups/20230115/images/photo2.jpg
           Content identical to original photo2.jpg (20 bytes)
       /home/user/remote_host/backups/20230115/scripts/cleanup.sh
           Content identical to original cleanup.sh (29 bytes)

    2. Directory /home/user/backup_logs/ exists and contains:

       /home/user/backup_logs/backup_20230115.log
           Content (exactly 65 bytes including LFs):
               docs/report.txt(LF)
               images/photo1.jpg(LF)
               images/photo2.jpg(LF)
               scripts/cleanup.sh(LF)

    The automated tests will checksum the copied files and verify the exact
    contents of backup_20230115.log.
